source_jobs.title: Account Executive - Enterprise (m/f/d)
source_jobs.description:
YOUR JOB

Are you a strategic closer with a passion for building strong, high-impact relationships? Do you thrive on securing game-changing deals and outperforming competitors in the enterprise landscape? If navigating complex sales cycles and winning large enterprise contracts excites you, then this is for you! With our multi-suite product portfolio in a fast-growing, in-demand space, you'll be at the forefront of offering innovative solutions that address diverse needs of security and compliance. Profit from target accelerators, dynamic deal multipliers, and strong existing logos to support your success, the stage is set—join us now and make your mark!



WE PROTECT THE PEOPLE BEHIND THE DATA

We are a fast-growing security and compliance tech company with a clear purpose: we protect the people behind the data. With over 200 team members worldwide and with offices in Munich, Berlin, London, Vienna and Stockholm we help more than 4,000 global customers get certified fast and build a strong and scalable risk posture. With AI-powered automation, self-serve capabilities and additional tailored expert advice, we offer our customers a seamless security and compliance experience to stay ahead of the rapidly evolving security landscape and challenges like the surge in cyber-attacks.



WHAT YOU'LL BE DOING

 - You expertly position DataGuard's multi-suite solutions to key decision-makers and C-suite executives, driving growth across the enterprise segment.

 - You lead and manage the entire enterprise sales cycle from prospecting to closing, ensuring a smooth handoff to our Customer Success Team for long-term client success.

 - You develop strategic account plans tailored to large organizations, identifying and unlocking high-value opportunities to expand our enterprise footprint.

 - You embrace a hunter mentality, leveraging a consultative, multi-touch approach to source leads, build a strong pipeline, and drive high-value enterprise deals.

 - You forecast revenue (ARR) with precision, providing clear visibility into the sales pipeline and contributing to our long-term growth strategy.

 - You consistently surpass your individual quota, redefining success in sales and climbing the career ladder quickly.



WHAT YOU'LL GET IN RETURN

 - The freedom, trust, and tools to do what you love and make an impact in a purpose-driven company, committed to doing genuine good in the digital world.

 - Enjoy a high degree of flexibility with tailored working hours, choice of work location, and benefits that support your lifestyle, health, and family needs.

 - Join a secure, future-focused role in a dynamic scale-up positioned to make a major impact in the rapidly expanding Security & Compliance market.

 - Grow along expert or leadership paths with support from employee accelerator programs, bi-annual feedback cycles, and a personal training budget.

 - Work in an environment that values autonomy, rapid decision-making, a flat hierarchy, and collaborative, eye-level communication to keep things moving forward.



THE QUALITIES YOU'LL NEED FOR A SUCCESSFUL CAREER AT DATAGUARD

 - University degree in business, economics, marketing or comparable.

 - At least 3 years experience in a B2B, SaaS closing role within the enterprise or midmarket segment.

 - Compelling sales track record with consistent quota achievement.

 - Proficient in lead generation via outbound.

 - Excellent skills in addressing business leaders and client-side functional managers involved in the decision-making process (MEDDIC).

 - Analytical skills combined with a getting-things-done attitude and enthusiasm, as well as a confident and poised demeanour.

 - Team player, willing to learn from others and to share own experiences.

 - German skills at native speaker level and fluent English skills.



LAST BUT NOT LEAST. FROM THE BOTTOM OF OUR HEARTS
No matter your origin, ethnicity, gender identity, religion or individual requirements; at DataGuard, all that counts is the pe

source_jobs.title: Customer Success Manager (m/f/d)
source_jobs.description:
YOUR JOB

Are you ready to excite customers and create real value? Do you love building close relationships and ensuring that customers make the most of our product's full potential? Do you want to promote long-term satisfaction and success? Then join the team and make a difference!





WE PROTECT THE PEOPLE BEHIND THE DATA

We are a fast-growing security and compliance tech company with a clear purpose: we protect the people behind the data. With around 200 team members worldwide and with offices in Munich, Berlin, London, Stockholm, and Vienna, we help more than 4,000 global customers get certified fast and build a strong and scalable risk posture. With AI-powered automation, self-serve capabilities, and additional tailored expert advice, we offer our customers a seamless security and compliance experience to stay ahead of the rapidly evolving security landscape and challenges like the surge in cyber-attacks.





WHAT YOU'LL BE DOING

 - You manage your own portfolio of medium-sized business customers and ensure they fully leverage the value of our solution

 - You build strong, long-term relationships, conduct product demos, and support customers in using our product optimally

 - You work closely with decision-makers or company founders, understand their priorities, and take proactive action to help them achieve their goals

 - You steer customers through the lifecycle and drive expansion and retention in your portfolio

 - You identify challenges early, lead solution-oriented discussions, and ensure long-term customer satisfaction

 - You analyze customer behavior and derive concrete actions to maximize their success with our solution

 - You foster engagement with and among customers, collect valuable success stories, and share best practices

 - You collaborate closely with teams such as Marketing, Sales, Product Development, and Tech Support to ensure a seamless customer experience





WHAT YOU'LL GET IN RETURN

 - The freedom, trust, and tools to do what you love and make an impact in a purpose-driven company, committed to doing genuine good in the digital world.

 - Enjoy a high degree of flexibility with tailored working hours, choice of work location, and benefits that support your lifestyle, health, and family needs.

 - Join a secure, future-focused role in a dynamic scale-up positioned to make a major impact in the rapidly expanding Security & Compliance market.

 - Grow along expert or leadership paths with support from employee accelerator programs, bi-annual feedback cycles, and a personal training budget.

 - Work in an environment that values autonomy, rapid decision-making, a flat hierarchy, and collaborative, eye-level communication to keep things moving forward.





THE QUALITIES YOU'LL NEED FOR A SUCCESSFUL CAREER AT DATAGUARD

 - Strong communication skills with a passion for product demos and meetings

 - Proactive, independent work style with a strong team spirit and excellent prioritization skills

 - Interest in compliance & security – we’ll teach you everything you need to know

 - Experience with software products and their development is a plus

 - Enthusiasm for a dynamic, fast-paced environment with continuous growth and development

 - Fluent German (C2) and English skills, both written and spoken, are required



LAST BUT NOT LEAST. FROM THE BOTTOM OF OUR HEARTS:

No matter your origin, ethnicity, gender identity, religion or individual requirements; at DataGuard, all that counts is the person you are. As Guards and Guardettes, we are united not only by our dedication, but also by our shared belief in our purpose: Protect the people behind the data. Convince us with your personality and your skills – and together we will make great things happen. We are looking forward to meeting you!

#LI-DNI

source_jobs.title: Account Executive (m/f/d)
source_jobs.description:
YOUR JOB

Are you a relentless deal hunter, energized by high-volume sales and driven by the thrill of competition? Does the challenge of closing contracts swiftly and securing the next big win keep you motivated? At DataGuard, you’ll be at the forefront of our hyper-growth journey, driving success with your sales expertise, determination, and collaborative spirit. With target accelerators, dynamic deal multipliers, and strong existing logos to support your success, the stage is set—join us now and make your mark!




WE PROTECT THE PEOPLE BEHIND THE DATA

We are a fast-growing security and compliance tech company with a clear purpose: we protect the people behind the data. With over 200 team members worldwide and with offices in Munich, Berlin, London, Vienna and Stockholm, we help more than 4,000 global customers get certified fast and build a strong and scalable risk posture. With AI-powered automation, self-serve capabilities and additional tailored expert advice, we offer our customers a seamless security and compliance experience to stay ahead of the rapidly evolving security landscape and challenges like the surge in cyber-attacks.




WHAT YOU'LL BE DOING

 - You strategically position DataGuard multi suite hybrid solution with decision makers, driving new business growth in the SMB and Midmarket segment

 - You manage the entire sales process, ensuring a seamless transition to our Customer Success Team

 - You craft compelling sales plans, unlocking untapped opportunities in your territory

 - You drive our revenue forward with a dynamic, multi-touch channel approach, always maintaining a robust pipeline

 - You document activities in our CRM system, ensuring data accuracy always

 - You forecast future revenue (ARR) with precision, contributing to long-term growth

 - You consistently surpass your individual quota, redefining success in sales and climbing the career ladder quickly




WHAT YOU'LL GET IN RETURN

 - The freedom, trust, and tools to do what you love and make an impact in a purpose-driven company, committed to doing genuine good in the digital world.

 - Enjoy a high degree of flexibility with tailored working hours, choice of work location, and benefits that support your lifestyle, health, and family needs.

 - Join a secure, future-focused role in a dynamic scale-up positioned to make a major impact in the rapidly expanding Security & Compliance market.

 - Grow along expert or leadership paths with support from employee accelerator programs, bi-annual feedback cycles, and a personal training budget.

 - Work in an environment that values autonomy, rapid decision-making, a flat hierarchy, and collaborative, eye-level communication to keep things moving forward.




THE QUALITIES YOU'LL NEED FOR A SUCCESSFUL CAREER AT DATAGUARD

 - University degree in business, economics, marketing or comparable

 - At least 2 years of experience in a B2B SaaS closing role

 - Compelling sales track record with consistent quota achievement

 - Proficient in lead generation via outbound

 - Excellent skills in addressing business leaders and client-side functional managers involved in the decision-making process (MEDDIC)

 - Analytical skills combined with a getting-things-done attitude and enthusiasm, as well as a confident and poised demeanour

 - Team player, willing to learn from others and to share own experiences

 - German skills at native speaker level as well as very good English skills



LAST BUT NOT LEAST. FROM THE BOTTOM OF OUR HEARTS.
No matter your origin, ethnicity, gender identity, religion or individual requirements; at DataGuard, all that counts is the person you are. As Guards and Guardettes, we are united not only by our dedication, but also by our shared belief in our purpose: Protect the people behind the data. Convince us with your personality and your skills – and together we will make great things happen. We are looking forward to meeting you!

source_jobs.title: (Senior) Accountant - Bilanzbuchhalter (m/f/d)
source_jobs.description:
YOUR JOB

Are you someone who thrives in fast-paced environments where accuracy, structure, and ownership truly matter? Your main goal will be to help build and scale our financial operations for the next stage of growth and take ownership of key accounting processes. If improving workflows, driving automation, and ensuring reliable financial reporting excites you - then this role could be the perfect next step.





LET'S PROTECT THE PEOPLE BEHIND THE DATA

We are a fast-growing security and compliance tech company with a clear purpose: we protect the people behind the data. With over 200 team members worldwide and with offices in Munich, Berlin, London, Vienna and Stockholm we help more than 4,000 global customers get certified fast and build a strong and scalable risk posture. With AI-powered automation, self-serve capabilities and additional tailored expert advice, we offer our customers a seamless security and compliance experience to stay ahead of the rapidly evolving security landscape and challenges like the surge in cyber-attacks.





WHAT YOU'LL BE DOING

 - You take ownership of key accounting processes including Accounts Payable, Accounts Receivable, monthly accruals, fixed asset management and payment runs, ensuring accuracy and efficiency at scale.

 - You take full ownership of month-end and year-end closing activities, delivering reliable, compliant financial reporting aligned with HGB requirements.

 - You lead the preparation of annual financial statements (HGB) and act as a key contact during internal and external audits.

 - You challenge existing workflows, proactively driving improvements and automation in collaboration with Finance and DataOps.

 - You take ownership of project-based financial initiatives and operational improvements together with our Director of Financial Controlling to strengthen our finance infrastructure as we grow.





WHAT YOU'LL GET IN RETURN

 - The freedom, trust, and tools (we work with DATEV) to do what you love and make an impact in a purpose-driven company, committed to doing genuine good in the digital world.

 - Enjoy a high degree of flexibility with tailored working hours, choice of work location, and benefits that support your lifestyle, health, and family needs.

 - Join a secure, future-focused role in a dynamic scale-up positioned to make a major impact in the rapidly expanding Security & Compliance market.

 - Grow along expert or leadership paths with support from employee accelerator programs, bi-annual feedback cycles, and a personal training budget.

 - Work in an environment that values autonomy, rapid decision-making, a flat hierarchy, and collaborative, eye-level communication to keep things moving forward.





THE QUALITIES YOU'LL NEED FOR A SUCCESSFUL CAREER AT DATAGUARD

 - German GAAP & Tax Law: You possess excellent knowledge of HGB and German tax regulations

 - You bring 3+ years of experience in financial accounting with end to end closing responsibility, including 1+ years experience in a high growth tech or SaaS company

 - Detail-Oriented & Reliable: You are detail-oriented, structured, and reliable - You have a proactive, adaptable, and ownership driven mindset with the ability to thrive in changing environments and successfully deliver projects from start to finish.

 - Communication Skills: You have excellent German skills and communicate clearly and confidently in English

 - Educational Background: Degree in Finance, Accounting, or related field, and certified training as Bilanzbuchalter

 - Strong proficiency in DATEV, Chargebee, and Excel, combined with a modern, tech driven mindset, solid AI literacy, and experience with systems such as Yokoy/Perk, Xero, Salesforce, or Adyen being a strong plus.

 - Nice to have: Experience with complex accounting topics such as VAT, transfer pricing, intercompany accounting, or experience in an international environment.



LAST BUT NOT LEAST. FROM THE BOTTOM OF OUR HEARTS:

No matter your origin, ethni

source_jobs.title: Events & Office Manager (m/f/d)
source_jobs.description:
YOUR JOB

Do you enjoy creating seamless workplace experiences that keep things running smoothly? Are you passionate about organizing events that bring people together and enhance company culture? Ready to gain hands-on experience in workplace operations while contributing to a dynamic and engaging work environment? Join our People Team and play a key role in making our office a great place to work!




LET'S PROTECT THE PEOPLE BEHIND THE DATA

We are a fast-growing security and compliance tech company with a clear purpose: we protect the people behind the data. With over 200 team members worldwide and with offices in Munich, Berlin, London, Stockholm, and Vienna, we help more than 4,000 global customers get certified fast and build a strong and scalable risk posture. With AI-powered automation, self-serve capabilities, and additional tailored expert advice, we offer our customers a seamless security and compliance experience to stay ahead of the rapidly evolving security landscape and challenges like the surge in cyber-attacks.




WHAT YOU'LL BE DOING

 - Thanks to your organizational talent you ensure clear structures and workflows to make DataGuard the “Best Place to Work” 

 - You are the first point of contact for day to day operations & office issues, from routine inspection rounds and facility management to supply ordering, deliveries and catering 

 - You support office expansion and relocation projects 

 - You bring in your ideas to plan and execute empowering team events on and off-site 

 - You maintain and foster a high-performing, innovative, appreciative, empowering, joyful, caring and on-site culture in the Workplace Operations team

 - You bring in ideas for AI powered tools and automation to simplify workplace operations, improve processes and create smarter, more efficient workflows
   
   


WHAT YOU'LL GET IN RETURN

 - Supported by a bi-annual feedback cycles, your development is at the forefront, helping you push your talents and climb the career ladder swiftly

 - Be part of a diverse, international, English-speaking company with experienced professionals by your side, dedicated to guiding your career growth and future success

 - Experience true autonomy with the freedom to make an impact, learn by doing, and find your own path—where trial and error are embraced as part of the journey

 - An EGYM Wellpass membership for your physical wellbeing, monthly financial support for local transportation needs, internet subsidy, and relocation assistance

 - DataGuard Flex Policy to strike the perfect balance between in-person collaboration, home office, or working from anywhere in the world

 - Regular organized company events, our annual off-site "Spikeweek," year-end celebrations, collaborative on-site sessions and team lunches
   
   


THE QUALITIES YOU'LL NEED FOR A SUCCESSFUL CAREER AT DATAGUARD

 - Organisational talent, ability to work under pressure and a quick learning ability 

 - Ideally, first working experience in a fast-moving environment 

 - Fluency in German and English to liaise with colleagues, customers and suppliers on a professional level 

 - Strong Microsoft Office skills 

 - High level of professionalism 

 - Proactive and strong communication skills 

 - You enjoy the diversity of tasks that enhance both your and the Guards'/Guardettes’ working day 
   
   

Please note: 20 hours/week, 3-5 days on-site in our Munich office



LAST BUT NOT LEAST. FROM THE BOTTOM OF OUR HEARTS: 
No matter your origin, ethnicity, gender identity, religion or individual requirements; at DataGuard, all that counts is the person you are. As Guards and Guardettes, we are united not only by our dedication, but also by our shared belief in our purpose: Protect the people behind the data. Convince us with your personality and your skills – and together we will make great things happen. We are looking forward to meeting you!  


#LI_DNI

source_jobs.title: Consultant Information Security (m/f/d)
source_jobs.description:
YOUR JOB

Do you want to rethink information security consulting with the help of AI? Are you excited to solve complex customer challenges while working smarter, faster, and at scale? Do you enjoy combining hands-on expertise with AI-powered automation to continuously improve how security is delivered? And do you thrive in an environment that encourages efficiency and challenges the status quo? Then join us on our journey.





WE PROTECT THE PEOPLE BEHIND THE DATA

We are a fast-growing security and compliance tech company with a clear purpose: we protect the people behind the data. With over 200 team members worldwide and with offices in Munich, Berlin, London, Stockholm, and Vienna, we help more than 4,000 global customers get certified fast and build a strong and scalable risk posture. With AI-powered automation, self-serve capabilities, and additional tailored expert advice, we offer our customers a seamless security and compliance experience to stay ahead of the rapidly evolving security landscape and challenges like the surge in cyber-attacks.





WHAT YOU'LL BE DOING

 - You actively shape DataGuard’s growth by managing and expanding the full customer lifecycle for our InfoSec-aaS product.

 - You advise customers on information security using our platform and build scalable ISMS solutions from scratch.

 - You act as a trusted partner, guiding customers through audits (e.g. ISO 27001 or TISAX®)

 - You leverage AI-powered tooling to deliver faster, more consistent, and higher-quality security consulting outcomes.

 - You support Sales and Customer Success with cross-selling and up-selling opportunities for existing customers.

 - You drive the evolution of our InfoSec solution by translating customer insights into actionable product feedback.





WHAT YOU'LL GET IN RETURN

 - The freedom, trust, and tools to do what you love and make an impact in a purpose-driven company, committed to doing genuine good in the digital world.

 - Enjoy a high degree of flexibility with tailored working hours, choice of work location, and benefits that support your lifestyle, health, and family needs.

 - Join a secure, future-focused role in a dynamic scale-up positioned to make a major impact in the rapidly expanding Security & Compliance market.

 - Grow along expert or leadership paths with support from employee accelerator programs, bi-annual feedback cycles, and a personal training budget.

 - Work in an environment that values autonomy, rapid decision-making, a flat hierarchy, and collaborative, eye-level communication to keep things moving forward.





THE QUALITIES YOU'LL NEED FOR A SUCCESSFUL CAREER AT DATAGUARD

 - Professional experience in IT and information security

 - Hands-on experience with ISO 27001, ISO 9001, SOC2, or TISAX®

 - Experience in customer-facing roles

 - Efficient, AI-driven working style with a mindset to continuously challenge the status quo

 - Strong process- and customer-oriented mindset

 - Up-to-date IT and information security knowledge

 - Interest in data protection topics

 - Excellent German and English communication skills



LAST BUT NOT LEAST. FROM THE BOTTOM OF OUR HEARTS:

No matter your origin, ethnicity, gender identity, religion or individual requirements; at DataGuard, all that counts is the person you are. As Guards and Guardettes, we are united not only by our dedication, but also by our shared belief in our purpose: Protect the people behind the data. Convince us with your personality and your skills – and together we will make great things happen. We are looking forward to meeting you!



*TISAX® is a registered trademark of the ENX Association. DataGuard has no business affiliation with ENX. We solely provide consulting and support services to help prepare for the TISAX® assessment. The ENX Association is not responsible for the content presented on the DataGuard website.



#LI-DNI

source_jobs.description:

Do you like combining consultative conversations with commercial responsibility? In this role, you’ll work closely with decision-makers in our customers, understand their priorities, and proactively develop their accounts. You’ll be the commercial owner of your portfolio, ensuring customers see continuous value and grow with DataGuard. source_jobs.title: Enterprise Customer Success Manager (m/f/d)
source_jobs.description:
YOUR JOB

Are you eager to be the strategic partner driving exceptional value for corporate accounts while building lasting relationships with top-level executives? Can you see yourself steering complex, multinational initiatives with precision, all while showcasing the measurable impact of our solutions on client success?






WE PROTECT THE PEOPLE BEHIND THE DATA

We are a fast-growing security and compliance tech company with a clear purpose: we protect the people behind the data. With over 200 team members worldwide and with offices in Munich, Berlin, London, Stockholm, and Vienna, we help more than 4,000 global customers get certified fast and build a strong and scalable risk posture. With AI-powered automation, self-serve capabilities, and additional tailored expert advice, we offer our customers a seamless security and compliance experience to stay ahead of the rapidly evolving security landscape and challenges like the surge in cyber-attacks.






WHAT YOU'LL BE DOING

 - You lead customer onboarding, ensuring seamless adoption through well-structured processes and customer enablement

 - You manage partnerships and stakeholders proactively, driving success, anticipating issues, and steering multiple initiatives using proven frameworks and tools

 - You understand customer needs by gathering insights into challenges and tailoring solutions to maximize satisfaction and business outcomes

 - You develop account plans, identify upselling and cross-selling opportunities, and collaborate with sales to drive customer expansion and retention

 - You build and maintain strategic partnerships by understanding client goals and aligning them with company solutions

 - You monitor customer portfolios, flag risks early, and create anti-churn strategies in collaboration with internal teams
   





WHAT YOU'LL GET IN RETURN

 - The freedom, trust, and tools to do what you love and make an impact in a purpose-driven company, committed to doing genuine good in the digital world.

 - Enjoy a high degree of flexibility with tailored working hours, choice of work location, and benefits that support your lifestyle, health, and family needs.

 - Join a secure, future-focused role in a dynamic scale-up positioned to make a major impact in the rapidly expanding Security & Compliance market.

 - Grow along expert or leadership paths with support from employee accelerator programs, bi-annual feedback cycles, and a personal training budget.

 - Work in an environment that values autonomy, rapid decision-making, a flat hierarchy, and collaborative, eye-level communication to keep things moving forward.






THE QUALITIES YOU'LL NEED FOR A SUCCESSFUL CAREER AT DATAGUARD

 - Proven experience as a Customer Success Manager, Account Manager, or Management Consultant, ideally in a fast‑paced SaaS environment

 - Strong track record in executive‑level meetings, stakeholder management, and delivering impactful presentations, demos, and project work

 - Genuine interest in Privacy, Information Security, and Compliance, with the motivation to continuously learn and adapt to evolving regulatory landscapes

 - Team‑oriented, action‑driven, and highly customer‑centric mindset

 - Excellent communication skills; fluent in English, German is a strong plus




LAST BUT NOT LEAST. FROM THE BOTTOM OF OUR HEARTS:

source_jobs.title: Channel Account Executive - Telekom (m/f/d)
source_jobs.description:
YOUR JOB

Help us drive one of our most strategic partnerships – with Deutsche Telekom. We’re looking for a highly motivated Channel Account Executive to own and expand our Tier 1 partnership with Deutsche Telekom. In this high-impact role, you’ll be the driving force behind our joint success - acting as the primary commercial link between our team and Deutsche Telekom’s regional sales organisation. Your mission will be to activate and scale joint go-to-market initiatives across Germany, fuel revenue growth, and create meaningful value for one of Europe’s most influential partners. This is a key role in our expansion across the DACH region. It calls for a sharp commercial mindset, strong collaboration skills in field sales environments, and the confidence to navigate a dynamic, multi-layered partner organisation.




WE PROTECT THE PEOPLE BEHIND THE DATA

We are a fast-growing security and compliance tech company with a clear purpose: we protect the people behind the data. With around 200 team members worldwide and with offices in Munich, Berlin, London, Vienna and Stockholm, we help more than 4,000 global customers get certified fast and build a strong and scalable risk posture. With AI-powered automation, self-serve capabilities and additional tailored expert advice, we offer our customers a seamless security and compliance experience to stay ahead of the rapidly evolving security landscape and challenges like the surge in cyber-attacks.




WHAT YOU'LL BE DOING

 - You act as the primary point of contact between our team and Deutsche Telekom’s field sales organisation at both regional and national levels.

 - You drive the execution of joint go-to-market strategies by collaborating closely with Deutsche Telekom’s sales representatives to position, co-sell, and close opportunities based on our shared value proposition.

 - You train and support the Telekom sales force, ensuring a comprehensive understanding of our platform, key use cases (e.g., NIS2, GDPR, ISO 27001), and deal qualification criteria.

 - You manage and grow the pipeline generated through Deutsche Telekom channels, ensuring alignment with forecasting, opportunity stages, and expected close dates.

 - You orchestrate enablement sessions, webinars, and account planning workshops tailored to the unique environment of Deutsche Telekom.




WHAT YOU'LL GET IN RETURN

 - The freedom, trust, and tools to do what you love and make an impact in a purpose-driven company, committed to doing genuine good in the digital world.

 - Enjoy a high degree of flexibility with tailored working hours, choice of work location, and benefits that support your lifestyle, health, and family needs.

 - Join a secure, future-focused role in a dynamic scale-up positioned to make a major impact in the rapidly expanding Security & Compliance market.

 - Grow along expert or leadership paths with support from employee accelerator programs, bi-annual feedback cycles, and a personal training budget.

 - Work in an environment that values autonomy, rapid decision-making, a flat hierarchy, and collaborative, eye-level communication to keep things moving forward.




THE QUALITIES YOU'LL NEED FOR A SUCCESSFUL CAREER AT DATAGUARD

 - Minimum 3–5 years of experience in channel sales and or partner account management

 - Experience with B2B SaaS or cybersecurity vendors preferred

 - Strong communication, organisational, and partner-facing skills

 - Data-driven approach and experience working with CRM and PRM tools

 - Native-level fluency in German and professional fluency in English



LAST BUT NOT LEAST. FROM THE BOTTOM OF OUR HEARTS.
No matter your origin, ethnicity, gender identity, religion or individual requirements; at DataGuard, all that counts is the person you are. As Guards and Guardettes, we are united not only by our dedication, but also by our shared belief in our purpose: Protect the people behind the data. source_jobs.title: Business Development Representative (m/f/d)
source_jobs.description:
YOUR JOB

Ready to take the next step in your Sales career?

We are looking for ambitious Business Development Representatives who already know the fundamentals of outbound sales and want to prove themselves in a new environment. In this role, you will help build qualified pipeline for our Upper Mid-Market segment through targeted outbound, account-based prospecting, multi-touch sequencing, and close collaboration with Marketing and Account Executives.

This is a high-impact role for someone with initial BDR experience who wants more ownership, sharper commercial exposure, and a clear path towards becoming an Account Executive. You will be expected to bring energy, structure, curiosity and resilience, while continuously improving how we identify, engage and convert the right prospects.

For strong performers, this role offers a clear development path into broader commercial responsibilities and future AE opportunities.





WE PROTECT THE PEOPLE BEHIND THE DATA

We are a fast-growing security and compliance tech company with a clear purpose: we protect the people behind the data. With over 200 team members worldwide and with offices in Munich, Berlin, London, Stockholm, and Vienna, we help more than 4,000 global customers get certified fast and build a strong and scalable risk posture. With AI-powered automation, self-serve capabilities, and additional tailored expert advice, we offer our customers a seamless security and compliance experience to stay ahead of the rapidly evolving security landscape and challenges like the surge in cyber-attacks.





WHAT YOU'LL BE DOING

 - Create qualified pipeline for our Upper Mid-Market segment through structured outbound prospecting, cold calling, email, LinkedIn, social selling and other creative engagement tactics.

 - Execute marketing-owned outbound and ABM motions, including targeted account research, multi-touch sequencing and personalised outreach.

 - Use tools such as Gong Engage, Salesforce and other sales technologies to manage sequences, track activity, improve messaging and prioritise the right accounts.

 - Research target accounts, understand buying signals and create relevant first conversations with senior stakeholders.

 - Qualify opportunities based on fit, urgency, business need and buying potential, then work closely with Account Executives to ensure a high-quality handover.

 - Act as a trusted first point of contact for prospective customers by clearly articulating DataGuard’s value proposition and business impact.

 - Bring your own initiative to pipeline creation, testing new messaging, account angles and prospecting approaches where you see opportunity.

 - Maintain accurate activity, account and pipeline data in Salesforce, using insights to improve prioritisation and performance.

 - Collaborate closely with Marketing, Sales, Revenue Operations and Enablement to improve outbound strategy, messaging, conversion and feedback loops.





WHAT YOU'LL GET IN RETURN

 - The freedom, trust, and tools to do what you love and make an impact in a purpose-driven company, committed to doing genuine good in the digital world.

 - Enjoy a high degree of flexibility with tailored working hours, choice of work location, and benefits that support your lifestyle, health, and family needs.

 - Join a secure, future-focused role in a dynamic scale-up positioned to make a major impact in the rapidly expanding Security & Compliance market.

 - Grow along expert or leadership paths with support from employee accelerator programs, bi-annual feedback cycles, and a personal training budget.

 - Work in an environment that values autonomy, rapid decision-making, a flat hierarchy, and collaborative, eye-level communication to keep things moving forward.





THE QUALITIES YOU'LL NEED FOR A SUCCESSFUL CAREER AT DATAGUARD

 - 9-15 months’ experience in a BDR, SDR, Inside Sales or similar customer-facing sales role, ideally in B2B SaaS.

 - A strong understanding of outbound fundamentals,

source_jobs.title: (Junior) Privacy Consultant - AI & Legal Tech (m/f/d)
source_jobs.description:
YOUR JOB

Are you excited by the idea of combining privacy, compliance and AI to solve real business challenges? Do you want to go beyond traditional consulting and help shape the next generation of technology enabled compliance? As a (Junior) Legal Engineer & Privacy Consultant, you advise clients on data protection and compliance while identifying opportunities to leverage AI, automation and Legal Tech solutions. Sitting at the intersection of law, technology and business, you help turn regulatory complexity into scalable, practical and future ready solutions.




WE PROTECT THE PEOPLE BEHIND THE DATA

We are a fast-growing security and compliance tech company with a clear purpose: we protect the people behind the data. With over 200 team members worldwide and with offices in Munich, Berlin, London, Stockholm, and Vienna, we help more than 4,000 global customers get certified fast and build a strong and scalable risk posture. With AI-powered automation, self-serve capabilities, and additional tailored expert advice, we offer our customers a seamless security and compliance experience to stay ahead of the rapidly evolving security landscape and challenges like the surge in cyber-attacks.




WHAT YOU'LL BE DOING

Privacy & Compliance Consulting

 - You advise and support clients on data protection, combining privacy expertise with smart use of our AI-powered platform.

 - You recommend and implement appropriate compliance measures, guiding clients to use DataGuard as their central privacy hub.

 - You design and roll out data protection policies and procedures that are efficient, scalable and sustainable.

 - You conduct data protection impact and risk assessments, leveraging automation to improve speed, clarity and consistency.

 - You contribute to platform and service development by sharing customer feedback and collaborating across teams.

AI & Legal Tech

 - You identify opportunities to automate legal, privacy and compliance workflows.

 - You build and improve AI powered assistants, workflows and automations that enhance consulting outcomes.

 - You explore emerging AI tools and technologies and evaluate their practical business impact.




WHAT YOU'LL GET IN RETURN

 - Steep learning curve with hands-on responsibility from day one

 - Close mentoring and strong support from experienced privacy consultants

 - Exposure to a wide variety of clients, industries and real-world use cases

 - Structured onboarding and continuous learning opportunities

 - Training budget and support for relevant certifications

 - High visibility across teams and insights into Product and Engineering

 - A collaborative, supportive team that values curiosity and growth

 - The chance to shape your career path as you grow with the company




THE QUALITIES YOU'LL NEED FOR A SUCCESSFUL CAREER AT DATAGUARD

 - You hold a degree in Law, Business Law or a related field.

 - You have gained first experience in privacy, compliance, consulting or Legal Tech, whether through internships, working student roles or full time positions.

 - You are curious about AI, automation and emerging technologies and have worked with tools such as ChatGPT, Claude and/or Microsoft Copilot.

 - You ideally have first exposure to APIs, n8n, Make or other low code tools.

 - You bring a structured, analytical and solution oriented mindset.

 - You enjoy working with customers and translating complexity into practical outcomes.

 - You are native-level fluent in German and comfortable working in English.



LAST BUT NOT LEAST. FROM THE BOTTOM OF OUR HEARTS:

No matter your origin, ethnicity, gender identity, religion or individual requirements; at DataGuard, all that counts is the person you are. As Guards and Guardettes, we are united not only by our dedication, but also by our shared belief in our purpose: Protect the people behind the data. Convince us with your personality and your skills – and together we will make great things happen. We are looking forward to me
